object: Arcane Spa / TileSpa (blockStoneDevice metadata 12), including its direct filled-container interaction, tank/inventory automation, GUI, recipe and research
scope_exclusions: Purifying Bath Salts internal expiry/warp-cleansing implementation was already closed in v11.64.14 and is only used here as an ingredient/output dependency
proved_discrepancies: The port always opened the GUI and omitted TC4's filled-container-first interaction, including whole-container consumption on a partial final fill.
The port saved Mix/Tank/Salts nested compounds instead of lowercase mix, root FluidStack and root Items used by TileSpa.
The port rejected replaceable positions containing a BlockEntity although TileSpa only checks top-solid support, replaceability and same-source exclusion.
The port consumed fluid/salts only after successful setBlock, while TileSpa consumes before the placement call and ignores its result.
The block used metal-like properties and non-original rarity instead of BlockStoneDevice rock hardness 3, resistance 25, stone sound and common rarity.
The GUI fluid strip width/phase and top masking differed from six full 8x8 tiles followed by the original 10px-wide empty-area mask.
New saves used incompatible key casing/layout and therefore did not directly interoperate with original TileSpa NBT semantics.
production_bindings: ArcaneSpaBlockEntity save/load -> exact TC4 keys plus legacy port migration
ArcaneSpaMenu/ArcaneSpaScreen -> exact slot, toggle, tank gauge and mask contract

resource_evidence.pedestal_bottom: byte-identical original pedestal_top.png used on bottom face

build_blocker: Gradle Wrapper cannot download Gradle 7.5.1 because services.gradle.org is unavailable in the execution environment; installed runtime is Java 21 rather than the required Java 17.
honesty: No BUILD VERIFIED or RUNTIME VERIFIED claim is made.
